claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION explicitly documents four valid evidence scopes
evidence.why: Schema contract defines the evidence scope field as project | external | user | unknown and states project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant declared as Set with four scope values
evidence.why: Code-level enforcement of the four valid scope values: project, external, user, unknown.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function distinguishes all four scopes with alias mapping
evidence.why: Runtime scope classification maps aliases (repo/repository/codebase/local to project, web/internet/research to external, human/operator to user) and returns unknown for unverified records.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator validates project evidence has repo-relative path
evidence.why: Project evidence requires a concrete file path that is not a URL; URLs auto-classify as external scope.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeSummary analyzes evidence records by scope and project locator presence
evidence.why: Aggregates scope distribution and flags whether evidence has project scope with concrete locator vs. project scope without path.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues blocks evidence records missing valid scope declaration
evidence.why: Readiness gate prevents evidence without explicit scope field from satisfying PRD requirements.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues enforces project evidence requires project-scoped records with paths
evidence.why: Blocks external-only or unknown evidence from satisfying existing-project facts; requires project scope with concrete path locator.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES declared as Set containing the four valid scope values
evidence.why: Dispatch-level validation uses the same four scope constants.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates each evidence record declares one of the four valid scopes
evidence.why: Agent result validation fails if evidence records do not declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent instructions with scope requirements
evidence.why: Prompt explicitly states every evidence record must include scope as one of the four values and project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules enumerate scope requirements and project evidence path requirements
evidence.why: Prompt-level enforcement tells agents that project facts require code/tests/docs/config/logs/artifacts with repo-relative paths.

evidence.summary: Required JSON shape in agent prompt documents the four scope values and source types
evidence.why: Agent output schema contract defines scope field as project | external | user | unknown and enumerates corresponding source types.

evidence.summary: Test verifies external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts
evidence.why: Contract test confirms that when all three agents return scope=external evidence, PRD readiness fails with PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker.

evidence.summary: Test verifies project evidence requires a concrete project locator
evidence.why: Contract test confirms that project-scoped evidence without a repo-relative path triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.

evidence.summary: Test verifies external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence
evidence.why: Contract test confirms that evidence with scope=project but a URL field triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.

evidence.summary: Test verifies evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly
evidence.why: Contract test confirms that evidence records without a scope field trigger EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, verifier protocols
evidence.why: Evidence agent roles are defined with objectives that require scope-aware evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks evidence agent results for scope compliance
evidence.why: Readiness contract validates each evidence result for scope declaration issues and project evidence path requirements.

risks: If scope validation logic fails to correctly classify evidence, existing-project claims could be satisfied by external research
If agents mislabel URLs as project evidence, the system must still detect and block them
If project-scoped evidence lacks repo-relative paths, PRD readiness incorrectly passes

result.notes: All four components—schema definition (router.ts:37-50), agent prompt (evidence-dispatch.ts:343-388), readiness contract (router.ts:438-530, 709-733), and tests (demand-router.test.ts:407-591)—explicitly distinguish and enforce the four evidence scopes. The implementation includes scope validation (router.ts:382-406), path requirements for project evidence (router.ts:408-419), and blockers for external-only evidence attempting to satisfy existing-project facts.
